hook: ChatGPT gave me a 14-step plan when I asked how to feel less overwhelmed. I needed 2 sentences.
caption:
ChatGPT gave me a 14-step plan when I asked how to feel less overwhelmed. I needed 2 sentences.

This is the thing nobody talks about with AI:

It doesn't know if you're a "give me the full picture" person or a "just tell me the next move" person.

So it guesses. And it usually guesses wrong.

The Overwhelmed Optimizer gets a listicle when they needed a anchor.
The Big-Picture Thinker gets bullet points when they needed context.
The Skeptic gets cheerleading when they needed pushback.

It's not that the AI is bad. It's that it's talking to a version of you that doesn't exist — some average of everyone who's ever typed a similar prompt.

BootFile fixes this by mapping how you actually think. Not your job title. Not your "communication style" from a dropdown menu. The real stuff — how you handle uncertainty, what makes you trust information, whether you need to see the reasoning or just the conclusion.

Once your AI knows that? The 14-step plan becomes 2 sentences.

The right 2 sentences.

What's the most "this AI clearly doesn't know me" response you've ever gotten? Drop it below 👇
